name: sim-flow-vscode
description: VS Code adapter for the sim-flow AI-assisted modeling orchestrator (DMF and DSF).

chatParticipants.name: sim-flow
chatParticipants.description: Drive the AI-assisted modeling flow (DMF and DSF).

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show the current flow, step, and gate status.

commands.name: runs
commands.description: List recent experiment runs. Optional filter args are forwarded to the CLI.

commands.name: gate
commands.description: Read-only gate validation for <step>. Defaults to the current step.

commands.name: advance
commands.description: Validate the gate and, if clean, mark the step passed and bump current_step.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset a step and cascade downstream gates. Usage: /reset <step>.

commands.name: step
commands.description: Start a work or critique session. Usage: /step <step-id>.work or /step <step-id>.critique.

commands.name: auto
commands.description: Run the entire flow unattended from the current step through the end (work -> critique -> advance per step). Optional --spec <path> seeds DM0; otherwise DM0.work is interactive and the rest is automated.

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize sim-flow state in the current workspace.

commands.title: sim-flow: Run Flow (CLI agent in terminal)

sim-flow.binaryPath.description: Absolute path to the sim-flow binary. Empty string falls back to $PATH, then to the bundled binary.

sim-flow.foundationRoot.description: Explicit path to the sim-foundation repository root. Passed to the CLI as --foundation-root. Empty string delegates to the CLI's own resolution.

enumDescriptions: Spawn a fresh CLI-agent session per step (per-step mode). User types `/exit` to end each step; orchestrator gates and advances automatically.

Keep one CLI-agent session alive for the whole flow (single-session mode). Dashboard buttons inject prompts / gate results / advance commands via a Unix control socket. User types `/exit` to end the entire flow; orchestrator lazy-respawns on the next dashboard command.
sim-flow.session.mode.description: Lifecycle for the interactive CLI-agent backend (`claude-cli` etc.). Ignored for chat-pane backends.

enumDescriptions: Orchestrator walks current_step to the end of the flow without user input. Each step's work + critique sub-sessions and the advance gate run back-to-back.
Orchestrator parks waiting for explicit dashboard commands (Run Step, Run Critique, Run Gate, Advance, Reset). Critique-clean alone never auto-advances; the user clicks Advance.
sim-flow.flow.stepMode.description: Initial step-axis mode passed to `sim-flow auto --step-mode <mode>` when the dashboard launches a session. The dashboard's step-mode toggle (between Connect and Disconnect) overrides this for the current session and emits a SetStepMode host event when a live session is attached.

sim-flow.llm.verbose.description: When OFF, the dashboard injects a `be concise` system message into every LLM request so models skip preamble, recaps, and hedging language. ON keeps the model's natural verbosity.

sim-flow.llm.source.description: Backend name passed to `sim-flow auto --llm-backend`; resolved entirely by the orchestrator. Built-in choices are `vscode`, `anthropic`, `openai`, `ollama`, `lmstudio`, `vllm`, `claude-cli`, `codex-cli`, `gh-copilot-cli`. You can also reference a user-defined server by its `name` from `sim-flow.llm.servers`; those entries override the conventional default host/port for `ollama` / `lmstudio` / `vllm` / generic openai-compat backends. The extension no longer dispatches LLM calls itself -- the value flows through to the Rust orchestrator which owns the backend client.

sim-flow.llm.servers.description: User-defined OpenAI-compatible LLM servers. Each entry adds a row to the dashboard's Source dropdown that maps to the matching `kind` agent with the entry's `host` + `port` overriding the conventional default. Use this to point sim-flow at a remote vLLM / Ollama / LM Studio host, or to keep multiple local servers configured side-by-side.

name.description: Human-readable label shown in the dashboard's Source dropdown (e.g. `vllm-bigbox`, `ollama-laptop`). Must be unique within the array.

kind.description: Backend protocol. `ollama` / `lmstudio` / `vllm` are convenience aliases for OpenAI-compat servers running those products' default paths; `openai-compat` is the generic fallback.

host.description: Hostname or IP address of the server.

port.description: TCP port the server listens on (Ollama 11434, LM Studio 1234, vLLM 8000).

model.description: Optional model identifier (overrides `sim-flow.llm.model` for this server).

modelFamilyId.description: Optional explicit model-family override for this server (for example `qwen3_6`, `kimi_vl_thinking`, `claude_messages`). When set, this server-specific value overrides the global `sim-flow.llm.modelFamily` setting.

runtimeProfileId.description: Optional explicit runtime-profile override for this server (for example `openai_compat_generic`). When set, this server-specific value overrides the global `sim-flow.llm.runtimeProfile` setting.

path.description: URL path prefix served by the OpenAI-compat shim. Default `/v1` works for every supported product. Override for non-standard mounts.

enumDescriptions: Each /step launches a fresh chat tab; the tab closes when the session ends.
Sticky tab per project; new sessions stream into the existing tab.
sim-flow.chat.scope.description: Reserved for Phase 9 polish; currently the chat-tab lifecycle follows VS Code's defaults.

sim-flow.llm.model.description: Model identifier passed to the configured source. Empty string delegates to the source's default.

sim-flow.llm.modelFamily.description: Optional explicit model-family override (`gemma4`, `qwen3_6`, `kimi_vl_thinking`, `claude_messages`, `generic_chat`). Empty string infers the family from `sim-flow.llm.model`.

sim-flow.llm.runtimeProfile.description: Optional explicit runtime capability profile override. Leave empty to use the selected source's default runtime contract (`openai_compat_generic`, `anthropic_messages`, `vscode_language_model`, ...).

sim-flow.llm.critique.source.description: Optional separate LLM source for critique sub-sessions only. When set, the extension routes critique LLM dispatches here while work sessions continue to use `sim-flow.llm.source`. Typical use: run work on a fast local model (e.g. `lmstudio` / `vllm`) and route critique to a stronger hosted model (`anthropic`) so reviews catch issues the work-side model misses. Empty string falls back to `sim-flow.llm.source` (single-backend behavior).

sim-flow.llm.critique.model.description: Model identifier for critique dispatches. Falls back to `sim-flow.llm.model` when empty. See `sim-flow.llm.critique.source`.

sim-flow.llm.critique.modelFamily.description: Optional model-family override for critique dispatches. Falls back to `sim-flow.llm.modelFamily` when empty.

sim-flow.llm.critique.runtimeProfile.description: Optional runtime-profile override for critique dispatches. Falls back to `sim-flow.llm.runtimeProfile` when empty.

sim-flow.llm.qa.source.description: Optional separate LLM source for idle-state Q&A turns (the side-conversation that fires when you type a message while a sub-session is parked). When set, conversational Q&A is routed here while work + critique stay on their own backends. Empty string falls back to `sim-flow.llm.source`.

sim-flow.llm.qa.model.description: Model identifier for Q&A dispatches. Falls back to `sim-flow.llm.model` when empty.

sim-flow.llm.qa.modelFamily.description: Optional model-family override for Q&A dispatches. Falls back to `sim-flow.llm.modelFamily` when empty.

sim-flow.llm.qa.runtimeProfile.description: Optional runtime-profile override for Q&A dispatches. Falls back to `sim-flow.llm.runtimeProfile` when empty.

sim-flow.llm.ollama.baseUrl.description: Base URL for the Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Include the `/v1` suffix.

sim-flow.llm.lmstudio.baseUrl.description: Base URL for the LM Studio OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Include the `/v1` suffix.

sim-flow.dashboard.openOnActivate.description: Automatically open the Flow Dashboard when a sim-flow project is detected in the workspace.

sim-flow.dashboard.experimentalUi.description: Use the experimental Flow Dashboard UI (now the default; the original UI is preserved behind this toggle for fallback during the transition).

sim-flow.dashboard.showFullyAutomated.description: Show the red end-to-end automated-flow button next to the manual Play / Stop controls. Hidden by default because the automated flow walks every step (DM0 → DM4b) without stopping for review and can burn significant LLM credits. Toggle this on (Settings tab) only when you genuinely want a hands-off run from a complete spec.

sim-flow.dashboard.verilogSimEnabled.description: When the Generate Verilog button runs, also instruct the agent to simulate the emitted RTL with the user-supplied simulator binary, diagnose any failures, and iterate the generated SystemVerilog until simulation matches the Foundation model. Requires `sim-flow.dashboard.verilogSimulatorPath` to be non-empty; otherwise the simulate-and-iterate section is omitted from the injected prompt.

sim-flow.dashboard.verilogSimulatorPath.description: Absolute path (or PATH-resolvable command) of a SystemVerilog simulator the agent may invoke when `sim-flow.dashboard.verilogSimEnabled` is true. Examples: `/usr/local/bin/verilator`, `/tools/synopsys/vcs/bin/vcs`, `/opt/modelsim/bin/vsim`. Leave empty to disable the simulate-and-iterate appendix even when the checkbox is on.

sim-flow.coverage.thresholdPct.description: DM3c line-coverage acceptance threshold (percent). The critique gate fails when measured coverage is below this value. Changes are written to `.sim-flow/config.toml::coverage.threshold_pct` for the active project (the Rust orchestrator reads from the TOML); other projects in the workspace retain whatever their own TOML says until you switch to them and re-save the setting.

enumDescriptions: Every reported module must clear the threshold.
Only the project-wide total must clear the threshold.
sim-flow.coverage.level.description: DM3c coverage acceptance level. `module` requires every reported module to clear `sim-flow.coverage.thresholdPct`; `total` requires only the project-wide aggregate. Written to `.sim-flow/config.toml::coverage.level` for the active project on change.

sim-flow.llm.maxParallelRequests.description: Cap on concurrent in-flight LLM Work sessions during plan-detail walks (DM2cd / DM3ad / DM4ad). `0` means no limit (every pending milestone stub fires at once). `1` forces the legacy serial path. Higher values fan out N stubs in parallel up to the cap. Has no effect on execution walks (DM2d / DM3b / DM3c / DM4b). Written to `.sim-flow/config.toml::llm.max_parallel_requests` for the active project on change.

sim-flow.verilog.enabled.description: When enabled, the chat panel shows the SystemVerilog conversion step rail (SV0 → SV3) underneath the DirectModeling rail and offers a 'Convert to SystemVerilog' continue action once DM4b has passed (which flips the project into the systemverilog-convert flow via `sim-flow convert-sv`). When disabled, DM4b is the terminal step.

sim-flow.chatPanel.showContextState.description: When enabled, the chat panel marks transcript turns that have been evicted from the orchestrator's prompt stack (via dedup, mutation invalidation, phase-boundary cleanup, or explicit summarization) with a red ✗ icon and an explanatory tooltip. The transcript itself always retains the full history regardless of this setting; this only controls whether the visual indicator is shown.

sim-flow.chatPanel.transcriptCap.description: Maximum number of transcript entries the host sends to the chat webview per state update. Older entries are dropped from the snapshot so the webview never re-renders an unbounded history. The full conversation is still persisted on disk and in host memory; this is purely a render-cap. The default (500) easily fills any viewport even when every step group is collapsed to its summary line. Set to 0 to disable the cap (legacy behavior: send the entire transcript on every update).

sim-flow.auto.maxWorkIterations.description: Per-session structural-gate iteration cap in automated mode. If a single work session has produced no fenced artifact write block for this many consecutive turns, the orchestrator flips to manual. Default raised 3 → 6 after the post-Phase-0 hardening pass: with thinking disabled, qwen3.6 retry-work sessions sometimes need extra turns to settle into a write.

sim-flow.auto.maxCritiqueIterations.description: Absolute ceiling on critique retries per step in automated mode. Even genuinely-progressing runs stop here — 10+ retries means something else is wrong (prompt, gate, model).

sim-flow.auto.maxCritiqueNoProgressItera.description: No-progress cap: stop auto retries when this many consecutive critique passes do NOT strictly decrease the gate-failing-finding count. Catches plateaus / oscillations early so we don't burn the absolute retry budget on a stuck model. Set to 0 to disable (the absolute cap then becomes the only signal).

sim-flow.auto.maxLlmRequests.description: Hard cap on TOTAL LLM requests per work / critique sub-session. Backstop against runaway loops the per-step caps don't catch. Default 500 covers a full 14-step DM flow with retries; was 50 before, which tripped runs that were otherwise progressing fine. 0 disables (NOT recommended).

sim-flow.auto.noPreamble.description: When ON (default), loads the response-shape convention into every session: tool calls first, prose last, no recap, no hedging. Cuts qwen3.6 / nemotron preamble that routinely consumes the full max_tokens budget. Turn OFF when debugging a model's reasoning -- the extra prose IS what you want then.

sim-flow.auto.healthcheck.description: Probe `<base_url>/models` before launching the auto run. Catches `vLLM is dead` / `wrong port` / `wrong base_url path` failures up front instead of N minutes in. Disable for backends that don't expose a `/models` endpoint.

sim-flow.auto.cargoTimeoutSeconds.description: Per-invocation timeout for orchestrator-side cargo runners (cargo build / fmt / clippy / test). Prevents a hung test or a wedged compiler from hanging the whole orchestrator. Wired through SIM_FLOW_CARGO_TIMEOUT_SECS for CLI runs.

sim-flow.debug.description: Debug log categories. Each enabled token writes to <project>/.sim-flow/logs/. `events` = parsed events both directions plus the full message stack on every LLM request. `raw` = JSONL lines on the wire. `llm` = vscode.lm dispatch detail (input messages, every chunk, errors). Empty disables logging. Setting overrides the SIM_FOUNDATION_DEBUG environment variable.

sim-flow.llm.debugAdaptation.description: When ON, sim-flow emits extra adaptation diagnostics around LLM dispatches and failures, including the active backend, runtime profile, model-family profile, and key capability flags.

scripts.compile:webview: esbuild src/webview/panel.ts --bundle --format=iife --outfile=dist/webview/panel.js --target=es2022 --log-level=warning && esbuild src/webview/panelExperimental.ts --bundle --format=iife --outfile=dist/webview/panelExperimental.js --target=es2022 --log-level=warning && esbuild src/chatPanel/panel.ts --bundle --format=iife --outfile=dist/webview/chatPanel/panel.js --target=es2022 --log-level=warning && esbuild src/chatPanel/panelExperimental.ts --bundle --format=iife --outfile=dist/webview/chatPanel/panelExperimental.js --target=es2022 --log-level=warning && esbuild src/perfPanel/panel.ts --bundle --format=iife --outfile=dist/webview/perfPanel/panel.js --target=es2022 --log-level=warning
scripts.compile:webview-types: tsc -p src/webview/tsconfig.json && tsc -p src/chatPanel/tsconfig.json && tsc -p src/perfPanel/tsconfig.json
scripts.copy:codicons: node -e "const fs=require('fs'),path=require('path');const src=path.join('node_modules','@vscode','codicons','dist');if(!fs.existsSync(src)){console.warn('copy:codicons: @vscode/codicons not installed (skipping; the committed copy in media/codicons/ will be used).');process.exit(0);}fs.mkdirSync('media/codicons',{recursive:true});for(const f of ['codicon.css','codicon.ttf'])fs.copyFileSync(path.join(src,f),path.join('media/codicons',f));"
scripts.compile: npm run codegen:protocol && npm run typecheck:extension && npm run copy:codicons && npm run compile:extension && npm run compile:webview-types && npm run compile:webview
scripts.ci: npm run compile && npm run lint && npm run format:check && npm run test
